flashcards.definition: c² = a² + b² − 2ab cos C; generalizes the Pythagorean theorem.
Two sides and the included angle → use Law of Cosines to find the third side.
Three sides → rearrange to find each angle: cos C = (a²+b²−c²)/(2ab).
When C = 90°, cos C = 0 and the Law of Cosines reduces to c² = a² + b².
Find the largest angle first (opposite the longest side) to determine if the triangle is obtuse.
